expectations.text: Generates a .docx file using python-docx (not markdown output)
expectations.evidence: Baseline also generated a python-docx script that produces a .docx file (qa_report_february_2026.docx). It also produced a .txt version as intermediate output.

expectations.text: Includes dimension scores (Greeting, Listening, Accuracy, Tone, Flow, Compliance, Resolution)
expectations.evidence: Baseline script includes scoring dimensions in the QA report, though the exact structure may differ from the skill template.

expectations.text: Identifies and flags calls that need attention (e.g., Call 3 angry caller, Call 10 data request)
expectations.evidence: The .txt output mentions flagged calls and analysis per call.

expectations.text: Includes actionable recommendations based on the call data
Uses proper formatting: title, headings, tables, not raw markdown
expectations.evidence: Baseline used python-docx with table formatting, though likely less polished than the skill version (no dark blue headers, shaded excerpts, etc. specified).

summary: Baseline also produced a functional .docx report. The key difference is likely in formatting polish and adherence to Inspra brand standards.
